items.headline: Council to discuss three lawsuits against Cupertino in closed session
items.summary: City Council is scheduled to meet in closed session on pending litigation including Lixin (Leon) Chen et al. v. City of Cupertino, Yes in My Backyard et al. v. City of Cupertino, and Garden Gate Coalition for Mary Avenue Safety v. City of Cupertino, all filed in Santa Clara County Superior Court.

items.headline: San Jose issues eight ADU permits in one week
items.summary: All eight building permits issued this week are for accessory dwelling units or junior ADUs — garage conversions, attached units, and detached backyard homes — spread across addresses from Lewis Road to Bundy Avenue. Costs range from about $60,000 to $132,000, showing how homeowners are using state ADU rules to add rental or family housing without new subdivisions.

items.headline: Council to consider pulling tobacco permit for California Cigar
items.summary: A public hearing is scheduled to consider revoking the business license and tobacco retailer permit for California Cigar.

items.headline: Council study session on updating General Plan safety element
items.summary: Council is scheduled to hold a study session on updating the Health and Safety Element of Cupertino's General Plan, which covers the city's 2015-2040 community vision.
